quotes.quote: I think that we'll be ready to make a final decision in the next week. So I think we're, we're definitely getting ready to make a final decision and move forward.
quotes.speaker: Emma Sternerup
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states they are ready to make a final decision and move forward within a week

quotes.quote: I think ideally if we could be launched by mid-August, which sounds like it might be kind of tight, but if we were to move forward quickly, it might be feasible.
quotes.speaker: Emma Sternerup
quotes.why: Customer expresses a concrete go-live target date, signaling urgency to close and implement quickly

quotes.quote: Yeah, I think everything you've described has met our expectations.
quotes.speaker: Emma Sternerup
quotes.why: Customer explicitly confirms that SimplerQMS has met all their expectations across key evaluation criteria

quotes.quote: He wants to launch on the 1st of October. That is the number one goal. And to go to launch, we need to be as far through validation as possible. And so that the validation is the critical piece. Without validation, we can't sell the product.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Hard launch deadline drives urgent need for the eQMS validation capability SimplerQMS provides

quotes.quote: Yes, I think so. Yeah, yeah, you are, yeah.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Confirms SimplerQMS pricing is in line with competing quotes, removing price as a barrier

quotes.quote: Yeah, that's what I'm hoping. Yeah, we really need to move on things. So yeah.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Expresses urgency to decide quickly after the 22nd internal meeting, signalling strong intent to proceed

quotes.quote: I think frankly, I think The other one was cheaper, but we kind of like yours a little bit better. So that's sort of why we're, why we're here. We can see what the best deal we can get, and then we're ready to, I think, make a decision. You're certainly, I would say, in the lead.
quotes.speaker: Mike Labbe
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states preference for SimplerQMS over cheaper alternative and names them as the frontrunner

quotes.quote: I think the team felt like this was probably the best product for us, so I'm sure I could sell that.
quotes.speaker: Mike Labbe
quotes.why: Customer confirms internal advocacy and confidence he can get team buy-in on the deal

quotes.quote: I mean, we're already pretty much ready to get going... I think, you know, within a week or two, whatever your availability is, we'd be ready to get going.
quotes.speaker: Mike Labbe
quotes.why: Customer signals immediate readiness to proceed and sets a concrete short-term start window

quotes.quote: I think we're pretty much ready to move forward. I mean, the proxy issue for us is not ideal, but I don't— I figure we can probably just update a procedure and do some workaround. It's not a showstopper, right? We understand that no product's going to fit 100% out of the box, more or less. But I mean, we're pretty close with you guys. So if that's our only little hangup, it's really not a deal breaker for us.
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states readiness to move forward and dismisses the only remaining concern as a non-deal-breaker

quotes.quote: it was very apparent that you guys have, exceptional customer service and support, especially with all those updates. So to us, it's, it's obvious that you guys are constantly taking that feedback and trying to improve the system for your users. So that was a huge selling point as well.
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer identifies a specific differentiating value that drove their decision to choose SimplerQMS

quotes.quote: my executive management look over the contract next week. So that should be putting us that first week in July. So it'll probably end up being the second week since it's a holiday and whatnot. So probably that second week starting with the 6th of July, somewhere in there.
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer provides a concrete implementation timeline, confirming active internal steps toward closing

quotes.quote: if we can get to, uh, $18K, uh, for first year, I can get a signature this week and then we could get an installation start towards contract already this week
quotes.speaker: Vaiva (COO, Cassini Technologies)
quotes.why: Customer commits to signing and starting implementation within the week if price condition is met, signaling strong and immediate buying intent

quotes.quote: on the one hand, we are happy to proceed. However, uh, to make sure that we can proceed quickly and then I get my CEO's signature quickly, we would need to go down in pricing a little bit
quotes.speaker: Vaiva (COO, Cassini Technologies)
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states they are happy to proceed and frames the only remaining obstacle as a pricing adjustment, confirming purchase decision

quotes.quote: since it is QMS, uh, I most likely don't want to switch it in a year, so I would be also fine with doing, uh, for now 2 years
quotes.speaker: Vaiva (COO, Cassini Technologies)
quotes.why: Customer voluntarily agrees to a multi-year commitment, indicating strong confidence in the solution's long-term fit

quotes.quote: my goal, Alan, is, is, is to either close it out today, close it out by tomorrow, right? Just get that out of the way because we need to get, we to get started here.
quotes.speaker: Danny Sala-Diakanda
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states intent to sign within 24 hours, signaling strong urgency to move forward

quotes.quote: I do want to get this moving fairly quickly. So obviously The more I can remove roadblocks, the better to get that going.
quotes.speaker: Danny Sala-Diakanda
quotes.why: Customer expresses urgency to accelerate the deal and actively remove obstacles to signing

quotes.quote: the last thing I want— I want to make sure we're on the same page here— the last thing I want is change because it puts the organization on pause to go ahead and do something like this.
quotes.speaker: Danny Sala-Diakanda
quotes.why: Customer signals strong commitment to proceeding with SimplerQMS, framing any disruption as undesirable

quotes.quote: The answer is yes, we like the product. And the pricing structure you mentioned fits for the reasons I mentioned. We like the approach.
quotes.why: Customer explicitly confirms product satisfaction and price fit, signaling strong purchase intent

quotes.quote: we need to push ahead on this and there's no point in turning it over for another 3, 6 months
quotes.why: Customer expresses urgency to move forward quickly rather than delay the decision

quotes.quote: Then if you can do that after the call, I'll get that back to you today.
quotes.why: Customer commits to returning the required company details form the same day, actively advancing the deal

quotes.quote: we agreed on that as long as the signature was, uh, was on, uh, this Monday, then we were good to go, right? That was, uh, that was what we agreed to on the phone.
quotes.speaker: Jonas Henriksen
quotes.why: Customer confirms a specific signing deadline already agreed upon, signalling imminent contract execution

quotes.quote: we also reached out to some people we know using SimpleQMS that you didn't refer to us, so, and we just had the same very positive impression.
quotes.speaker: Jonas Henriksen
quotes.why: Customer independently sought and received unsolicited positive references, reinforcing confidence in the product

quotes.quote: we like the— since we're very small, we like the sort of small-to-small interface instead of going with Viva or Master Control or some of them.
quotes.speaker: Jonas Henriksen
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states preference for SimplerQMS over named enterprise competitors Veeva and MasterControl

quotes.quote: you guys were our number one pick both times, so we would like to, you know, to keep that partnership
quotes.speaker: Amanda Solomon
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states SimplerQMS was top choice in both evaluations, confirming strong preference over all competitors

quotes.quote: Amanda and I are gonna push this as hard as we can to get this, because we, we want this solution as soon as possible
quotes.speaker: Kevin Laverty
quotes.why: Champion signals internal urgency and commitment to accelerate the buying process

quotes.quote: the price that you guys gave us is the reason why we were able to go ahead and get that
quotes.speaker: Amanda Solomon
quotes.why: Customer confirms SimplerQMS pricing was the deciding factor that secured executive approval over the competing ServiceNow option

quotes.quote: based on sort of this conversation today, you guys definitely step out, you know, in front, related not only to this, but to some of the costing flexibility in terms of the licensing and how that stuff is set up
quotes.speaker: Rachel Bedenbaugh
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states SimplerQMS leads over other vendors evaluated, citing both fit and pricing flexibility

quotes.quote: I think that a lot of our needs, all of our needs that we brought up have been met through this software, and you guys have answered our questions pretty well
quotes.speaker: Zack DiSanza
quotes.why: Customer confirms all requirements are satisfied by the solution, signaling strong product fit

quotes.quote: we don't see ourselves moving away from BenchLink, like not in the near future
quotes.speaker: Priya Luthra
quotes.why: Customer confirms long-term reliance on a key integration target, validating the integration path with SimplerQMS as a lasting need

quotes.quote: what i have seen in the demo from you is what i really liked i mean i could i could totally imagine working with the system
quotes.speaker: Johannes Riebl
quotes.why: Prospect explicitly states he can envision himself using the product, confirming strong product fit

quotes.quote: I think we could sign a contract for 24 months. I think, Nicole, that would be realistic because we don't want to change. I mean, when we move everything over there and then we don't want to change quickly, right, without any reason. So I think that should be absolutely feasible.
quotes.speaker: Johannes Riebl
quotes.why: Prospect commits to a 24-month contract term, signalling clear intent to proceed

quotes.quote: I'm quite confident that we can take a decision within April.
quotes.speaker: Johannes Riebl
quotes.why: Prospect sets a concrete decision deadline, indicating active buying momentum

quotes.quote: No, I think that's really helpful because for us, it's a bit difficult to think how it will be done when we change the system from Windows Explorer to such a database.
quotes.speaker: Lisa Frisch
quotes.why: Customer explicitly affirms the demo is helpful and frames the conversation around their transition plan, signaling fit and forward-looking intent
quotes.company: Zedira (referred to as Sidero in notes)

quotes.quote: yeah, now we need to decide which system we want to take
quotes.speaker: Lisa Frisch
quotes.why: Customer states they are at the decision stage, indicating readiness to buy
quotes.company: Zedira (referred to as Sidero in notes)

quotes.quote: Excel and Word, so it works the same way.
quotes.speaker: Lisa Frisch
quotes.why: Customer confirms the core functionality meets their workflow needs, signaling acceptance of the solution
quotes.company: Zedira (referred to as Sidero in notes)

quotes.quote: We will say that, yes, we narrowed down simpler.
quotes.speaker: Vaibhav Mantri
quotes.why: Customer explicitly confirms SimplerQMS has been selected as the preferred vendor over other evaluated options

quotes.quote: I felt it is like easy, like quickly, like I can learn quickly, that's what I had feeling, it is easy to use.
quotes.speaker: Vaibhav Mantri
quotes.why: Customer affirms the platform's ease of use as a positive differentiator supporting their selection

quotes.quote: I will get this out now, guys. Hopefully I get a response so we can get this thing back.
quotes.speaker: Ernesto Samuel
quotes.why: Customer expresses intent to move the process forward immediately by contacting his legal team to finalize the deal

quotes.quote: I personally would love it to be in one system, so we don't have to go buy a separate system, separate software, right? I mean, that's not optimal in my mind. So as long as it's functional or, you know, and feasible and et cetera, then I think it's good.
quotes.speaker: Dan Holsworth
quotes.why: Explicitly states preference for a single system and signals acceptance if SimplerQMS meets functional needs

quotes.quote: we plan on starting phase three, first quarter of 27, you know, at the latest. I mean, so we'll be ready with IP in the fourth quarter of 26, at the beginning of that fourth quarter.
quotes.speaker: Jd
quotes.why: Concrete phase three start date creates a clear implementation deadline that drives urgency to select a solution

quotes.quote: sounds reasonable
quotes.speaker: Ed Mar
quotes.why: A typically reserved stakeholder explicitly endorses the solution, signaling broad internal alignment

quotes.quote: Oh, exactly. Yeah, exactly. Make, make a choice by then anyway.
quotes.speaker: Herman (Thomas's contact at Pyure)
quotes.why: Customer confirms they will make a purchase decision within the trial period, signaling imminent buying intent.

quotes.quote: So I have to— you go to the— I believe it's go up or go down. You have one. Oh, down. The difference between the full and the standard. Yeah, I want to go in the standard here. So I have to pick one of the— of these 2, 2
quotes.speaker: Herman
quotes.why: Customer is actively evaluating specific license tiers for their team, indicating they are in a decision-making mindset ready to select a plan.

quotes.quote: Sounds good. Sounds good. Okay.
quotes.speaker: Herman
quotes.why: Customer explicitly affirms satisfaction with the full Office integration (PowerPoint, Excel, Word) capability, confirming product fit.

quotes.quote: we're sort of in an in-between spot where we don't need to have a QMS at the moment, but quite soon we will need one
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states they will need a QMS soon, signalling imminent purchase intent

quotes.quote: that's great. So I see here that you, uh, it's functional in MS Office apps
quotes.why: Customer affirms a key feature fit with their existing toolset, indicating the solution meets their needs

quotes.quote: recap of cost and, uh, and, and discount, discounts of course most importantly, but what, what, what, what really, what is, what is included in the package you, you're selling as, as standards
quotes.why: Customer proactively revisits pricing and package details, signalling readiness to move toward a purchase decision

quotes.quote: I'm more comfortable with Office than I am with Greenlight. It was always, if I went away from Greenlight for a period of time and came back to it, I'd have to almost get used to it again, relearn it, and yeah, it was, but I like some aspects of it, but no, I think going to the Office environment could happen relatively easy.
quotes.speaker: Jeff Chalpin, Director of Engineering
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states preference for SimplerQMS's Office-based approach over Greenlight Guru, lowering perceived switching friction

quotes.quote: I think it would just be an adjustment to switch to the office environment to take care of that. But I think with some time we could get it set up so it wouldn't be difficult to do.
quotes.speaker: Jeff Chalpin, Director of Engineering
quotes.why: Customer confirms the solution is workable for their design workflow, signaling acceptance of the product fit

quotes.quote: Sure. Yeah, absolutely.
quotes.speaker: Danny Sala, CTO and COO
quotes.why: CTO/COO explicitly agrees to proceed to pricing discussion, indicating executive-level readiness to move forward

quotes.quote: this is something that I mentioned, that is, this is taking like six or eight weeks, so the clock is ticking on here, so whatever we're going to do, so they know that part, so yeah, in my opinion, this is something that we should be taking a decision very soon
quotes.speaker: German Fernandez
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states the clock is ticking and a decision must come very soon due to implementation lead time

quotes.quote: this is very positive, you know, because you're doing the change in the same way. So you are connected with Office, basically.
quotes.speaker: German Fernandez
quotes.why: Customer confirms SimplerQMS's Office integration solves a key pain point they had with GreenLight Guru

quotes.quote: I know you do the full process. So you have, and you, you provide the report.
quotes.speaker: German Fernandez
quotes.why: Customer acknowledges and affirms the included validation service as a clear benefit over their current situation

quotes.quote: that is nothing— that is, uh, that's not an issue.
quotes.speaker: Kristina Everbrink
quotes.why: Customer explicitly dismisses the price increase to $15,500 as a non-issue, confirming budget acceptance

quotes.quote: If we decide upon that, we would like to sign the agreement in quite a short time. I have heard that it would be great if we'd done this before summer, so we are pretty close.
quotes.speaker: Kristina Everbrink
quotes.why: Customer states intent to sign soon with a self-imposed pre-summer deadline, signalling strong purchase urgency

quotes.quote: So this is as good as I recalled it. I looked into this with my colleague Julia, and we were just like, oh, this is great. This is what we want.
quotes.speaker: Kristina Everbrink
quotes.why: Customer confirms the product meets their needs and references internal stakeholder alignment, directly signalling fit and intent

quotes.quote: we're really leaning towards your system. We're super impressed with it.
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer explicitly states strong preference and positive impression of SimplerQMS, signaling high purchase intent.

quotes.quote: So say we decide to go through with this. What does that look like?
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer proactively asks about next steps and implementation, indicating readiness to move forward.

quotes.quote: if we're good to go and there's no red flags from anyone else, then I'll probably reach out to you guys mid-next week and maybe then we can move ahead with talking implementation and things like that
quotes.speaker: Nicole DeWald
quotes.why: Customer sets a concrete follow-up timeline and explicitly mentions moving ahead with implementation, signaling imminent decision.

quotes.quote: we got the go-ahead there. So, um, so yeah, we got, um, the go-ahead there. So, um, I was pleased about that, um, had buy-in from everyone, and, um, and SimplerQMS were the, were the, the top choice throughout all of our analysis.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Confirms internal approval and unanimous stakeholder buy-in with SimplerQMS selected as top choice

quotes.quote: what tipped it really was the turnaround time, because most of them were double your maximum time. So, and we've just not really got that time to— so, yeah, it was the timing really that swung it for us.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Explicitly states SimplerQMS's faster implementation time was the decisive factor over competitors

quotes.quote: Yeah, I would go for 24 months and that would, that would get us started and Yeah.
quotes.speaker: Alison Engles
quotes.why: Customer verbally commits to a 24-month agreement, signalling clear purchase intent
